# Letter to Menoeceus

*[Epicurus](../philosophers/epicurus.md) · 300 BCE*

A brief letter in which [Epicurus](../philosophers/epicurus.md) sums up his ethics of happiness: freeing oneself from the fear of the gods and of death, classifying one's desires, and making [pleasure](../glossary/hedonism.md) understood as the absence of disturbance the goal of life. It is the most accessible text of Epicurean ethics.
